title: Thibaudia subcrenulata
taxonomy: Thibaudia subcrenulata is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It belongs to the genus Thibaudia, which is a group of flowering plants native to Central and South America. The Ericaceae family, to which Thibaudia subcrenulata belongs, is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as heath or heather plants. Within the Ericaceae family, Thibaudia subcrenulata is classified as a member of the order Ericales and the class Magnoliopsida. This classification reflects its relationships to other flowering plants and its evolutionary history.

intro: Thibaudia subcrenulata is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae, native to the Andes mountain range in South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and digestive issues. Studies have identified the bioactive constituents of Thibaudia subcrenulata, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
